mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal over a 4-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across durable and non-durable consumer demand sectors, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines with p < 0.05 over 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during inventory accumulation periods, yielding negative returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.6% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over 3-year walk-forward periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, providing a fundamental earnings tailwind that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDP growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 across all tested sub-samples.

mechanism_narrative: Rising policy rates coinciding with accelerating industrial production signal an overheating economy where monetary tightening outpaces supply growth, triggering valuation compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal yields positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances during tightening phases over a 3-year rolling window.
